title: File Permissions Repair
description: A web app config file has broken permissions. SSH in, inspect ownership and mode bits, then restore read access without changing the file contents.
tasks: SSH into the lab using the Lab Terminal
Repair permissions so the file is readable (mode 644)
Verify you can read the file with cat

objectivesPublic.label: SSH into the lab
objectivesPublic.text: Connect from the Lab Terminal using your session credentials.
objectivesPublic.hint: Use the credentials panel — the integrated terminal is already set up for this session.

objectivesPublic.label: Inspect the web app config
objectivesPublic.text: Locate the config under /var/www and check who can read it.
objectivesPublic.hint: Long listings and stat both show owner, group, and permission bits without changing anything.

objectivesPublic.label: Restore safe read access
objectivesPublic.text: The app needs the config readable again without editing its contents.
objectivesPublic.hint: Owner should keep read/write; group and others typically need read-only — express that as a three-digit mode.

questions.prompt: Which chmod mode gives the owner read+write and everyone else read-only access?
